title: Full 3D scene model with cross-sections

dimsim-spatial-memory.reason: Not exported for this recording yet.

human-activity.reason: Static-structure reconstruction; not the activity scenario's focus.

summary_html: SceneModel fuses everything a mapping session produced — the lidar cloud and robot trajectory from a Rerun recording, plus (optionally) the recorded camera stream for color — into one queryable 3D model of the space, sliceable into horizontal plan cuts and vertical elevations for viewing and analysis. The foundation item 1's floorplan generation builds on.

figures.alt: Left: 3D scatter of a synthetic two-story house point cloud with a horizontal cut plane at z=1.5 and a vertical cut line. Middle: the plan cut showing the four walls, stair volume and no table. Right: the elevation showing two floor slabs, roof, table and stair volume.
figures.caption_html: The real class run on a synthetic two-story house (42k points). Left: the model with both cut planes indicated. Middle: horizontal_section(z=1.5) — walls and stair volume, no low furniture. Right: vertical_section() across both stories — slabs, roof, table, stairs. Regenerate: uv run python misc/skills_dashboard/scripts/gen_scene_model_figures.py
figures.heading: What it does — plan cuts and elevations from one model

how_it_works.title: Ingest
how_it_works.html: SceneModel.from_rrd() walks the recording's chunks collecting every Points3D batch (the map) and LineStrips3D strip (the trajectory), then clips z outliers by percentile. The shared loader load_rrd_points() is what item 1's floorplan script now uses.

how_it_works.title: Colorize from imagery
how_it_works.html: colorize_from_session() correlates the session's stamped poses (gtsam_odom.tum) with the recorded color_image stream (mem2.db), projects sampled frames through the calibrated camera (camera_intrinsics.json), and paints each point from its closest view (nearest-view-wins z-test). 50% of the 6.4M-point china-office cloud colorized from 84 frames in ~30 s.

how_it_works.title: Slice
how_it_works.html: horizontal_section(z) and vertical_section(p1, p2) rasterize a thick planar cut into a Section — hit-count density plus mean RGB where colorized — with world-meter extents. An optional bounds window makes multiple sections cell-aligned so they can be combined directly: item 1's floorplan builds all of its per-level evidence rasters (occupancy / structural / ceiling bands) exactly this way.

how_it_works.title: Voxelize & seal (new)
how_it_works.html: voxel_occupancy(voxel=0.1) bins the cloud into a dense (nz, ny, nx) hit-count grid — the analytic 3D voxel model (~20 MB for a whole office building). VoxelOccupancy.closed_region() then seals each connected area the robot drove through: progressive morphological closing (0.3 → 2.5 m kernels, smallest that seals wins) with a leak test against the raster border, optionally bounded by a caller barrier (item 1 passes its ceiling-evidence indoor mask so ground floors don't leak through entrances). Yields a watertight interior, closed room-loop polygons, and bridged — the cells the closing inferred (glass, unscanned gaps).

how_it_works.title: Export 3D
how_it_works.html: save_rerun() writes an interactive colored model (dimos-viewer model.rrd); save_rerun_sliceable() additionally logs one horizontal section per z-step on a cut_z timeline, so the viewer's time slider sweeps a cut plane through the property (used by item 1's --viewer); to_mesh() runs Poisson surface reconstruction with low-support trimming and save_mesh() exports GLB/glTF/OBJ/PLY/STL with vertex colors — drops straight into Blender/three.js/CAD.

test_descriptions.test_horizontal_section_shows_walls_not_: A 1.5 m plan cut sees the room's walls but not 0.8 m furniture
test_descriptions.test_horizontal_section_at_table_height_: A 0.78 m cut picks up the table blob in the room center
test_descriptions.test_vertical_section_profile: An elevation across the room shows floor, both walls, and empty air above the table
test_descriptions.test_empty_section_is_well_formed: A cut through empty space returns a well-formed empty Section, never an error
test_descriptions.test_section_save_png: Section renders and writes a valid PNG
test_descriptions.test_quat_identity_and_yaw: Quaternion→matrix helper is correct for identity and yaw rotations
test_descriptions.test_colors_default_uncolored: Without imagery, no point claims a color and sections carry no color layer
test_descriptions.test_save_mesh_formats: Poisson mesh exports to GLB, OBJ and PLY (format by suffix)
test_descriptions.test_sections_with_shared_bounds_are_ali: Sections with a shared bounds window are cell-aligned and combinable
test_descriptions.test_voxel_occupancy_dims_and_origin: Voxel grid has the right shape/origin; wall columns occupied, room air empty
test_descriptions.test_voxel_slab_aligns_with_section: A voxel slab and a horizontal_section over the same bounds agree on >90% of cells
test_descriptions.test_closed_region_seals_gap: A 1 m unscanned wall opening is bridged (≤1.2 m kernel) and the interior no longer leaks
test_descriptions.test_closed_region_interior_area: Sealed interior area matches the room's true 24 m² within 15%
test_descriptions.test_room_loops_closed: Every room loop is explicitly closed (first == last) with the right enclosed area
test_descriptions.test_closed_region_intact_room_no_bridgi: A fully scanned room seals at the smallest kernel with ~zero inferred cells (over-closing guard)
test_descriptions.test_save_rerun_sliceable: The sliceable .rrd (cut_z timeline) is written and non-trivial
test_descriptions.test_scene_model_sections_and_exports: Skill-verification: a synthetic two-story house yields non-empty horizontal + vertical section rasters and a mesh (.glb) + sliceable .rrd; saves a plan/elevation figure for review

tests_note_html: the last row is from the skill-verification suite (dimos/skills/skill_verification), which builds the real SceneModel end-to-end and writes the section figure, mesh and .rrd to test_outputs/ (indexed by REVIEW.md)

examples.pre_html: from dimos.mapping.reconstruction.scene_model import SceneModel model = SceneModel.from_rrd("chinaOffice.rrd") model.colorize_from_session("~/session_dir") # camera → RGB points plan = model.horizontal_section(z=1.6) # plan cut elev = model.vertical_section((5, -35), (10, 25)) # elevation model.save_rerun("model.rrd") # dimos-viewer model.rrd model.save_mesh("model.glb") # or .obj/.ply/.stl
examples.caption: scripts/demo_rrd_3d_model.py wraps exactly this flow; on the china-office scan it produced 3 plan cuts, a full-building elevation, and a 509k-triangle colored GLB in under a minute

next_step_html: Wrap as a callable skill ("model the space seen so far", "show me a section at z=4.8"). Item 1's migration is complete — the floorplan pipeline slices this model first, then draws.

skills_note: no agent skill yet — library API: SceneModel / VoxelOccupancy in dimos/mapping/reconstruction/scene_model.py (demo CLI: scripts/demo_rrd_3d_model.py); next step wraps "model the space" / "show me a section at z" as skills
